subject: English / First Flight
chapter_name: Poems – Dust of Snow & Fire and Ice
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the themes, symbols, and deeper messages of both poems
Analyze the use of literary devices such as imagery, symbolism, and tone
Develop critical thinking by comparing the two poems
Interpret the poems’ messages in relation to human emotions and nature
Encourage creative expression and reflection on personal experiences

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Poets and Poems
topics_covered: Background and context of both poems
Overview of poem themes and structure
teaching_strategy: Discuss Robert Frost’s style and use of nature in poetry
Engage students in predicting themes based on titles
student_activities: Brainstorm what 'Dust of Snow' and 'Fire and Ice' might mean symbolically
Share previous experiences with nature or strong emotions
assessment: Ask students to identify the poet and poems
Oral Q&A on predictions and prior experiences

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Comprehension – Dust of Snow
topics_covered: Identify the main event and emotional change
teaching_strategy: Encourage visualization of the crow, snow, and tree
assessment: Ask students to explain how the poet’s mood changes
Check understanding through short written summaries

lesson_plan.title: Analysis – Dust of Snow
topics_covered: Theme: small events and their impact on mood
Tone and mood: from gloom to uplifted
teaching_strategy: Discuss symbolism and its relation to theme
Relate poem to personal experiences of small joys
student_activities: Identify symbols and explain their meanings
Discuss the poem’s moral lesson in pairs
assessment: Short oral responses on symbolism and theme
Written response: how a small event has affected their day

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Analysis – Fire and Ice
topics_covered: Discuss the symbolic meanings of fire and ice
Theme: destructive power of desire and hate
teaching_strategy: Line-by-line reading with discussion of emotions
Compare human emotions to natural forces
student_activities: Highlight lines representing desire (fire) and hate (ice)
Discuss tone and mood of the poem
assessment: Oral Q&A on symbols, theme, and tone
Short written response summarizing the poem

lesson_plan.title: Comparative Discussion and Creative Activity
topics_covered: Compare and contrast themes of both poems
Reflect on nature’s influence vs. human emotions
Creative expression: relate poems to personal experience
teaching_strategy: Encourage group discussion on similarities and differences
Guide students to create visual or written responses
student_activities: Write a paragraph connecting personal experience to either poem
Draw a scene or symbolic representation of key ideas from both poems
assessment: Class discussion on insights and interpretations

teaching_aids: Printed or projected texts of both poems
Images of crows, hemlock trees, fire, and ice
Charts for theme, symbols, tone, and mood
Flashcards with literary devices and keywords
teacher_tip: Encourage deeper thinking by linking symbols to emotions and personal experiences. Highlight differences and similarities in the poems’ approach to human feelings and nature. Use interactive activities to maintain engagement.
